Let me just write it here using pink color. So first layer is staging layer. Let's talk about this first. Staging layer is your first layer that you use in your ETL process. What will happen in the staging layer? In the staging layer, you just push the data in as is form like as it is just replica of the source and you just put the data here in the staging layer. Now we have four different types of uh storage chain dimension. Type 0, type one, type two, type three. By the way, we have type six like we have till type six as well. But these are the popular ones. Type 0 is like when we do not make any changes that means we are assuming our dimension will not be changing with the time. So this is called type zero. This is simple and this is a type that we have currently worked on. Then we have something called as type one. It just it it is commonly called as upsert. Upsert means update plus insert. Okay, it simply says let's say this is your product product dimension. Okay, and this was the name called com and the product category was clothing. But after some time, let's say after a after a few months, your company decide decided that you will be putting this particular com in a different category. they have launched a different category. So in this we simply update the values simply update the values do not need to worry anything just simply update the values and if we have let's say more values let's say four and we have let's say Maggie so product category will be let's say food and this is a new category that your organization has launched so simply you will insert it this and this see so you will simply update or insert that's called upsert this is the concept behind type one. LRS means locally redundant storage. That means your data will be replicated within the same data center. So it is called locally redundant storage. Now second one is ZRS zone redundant storage. That means your data will be replicated outside the data center and obviously within one zone there will be one data center. So that is why it is called zone redundant data storage or let's say storage policy whatever you want to say concept remains the same because interview can trick you using different different names. Then we have GRS. This means it will simply replicate your data within the region. So let's say in one region you have three zones. Okay. So that is your ZRS zone redundant storage. And then in the second region you have three more zones. So GRS means like your geo redund redundant storage means your one region will be replicated with the other region. Make sense? So when you just write your query any uh you can say code any transformation that gets first converted into optimized logical plan you know this okay then it gets converted into physical plan and before AQE that was the final plan but after aqe it is not the final plan. It will check the query statistics during runtime and if it feels that hey I can just change the join strategy now because now data is not much big enough it can change it. So, we have the option of data redundancy. That means we can store data, we can duplicate our data into different regions, locations. So that if one location is not available, second location is available. So within this we have three options. First of all, we have something called LRS. That's mean that means locally redundant storage in which let's say we have one data store let's say we have one data store data center sorry let's say we have one data center so within one data center we have different different data stores or racks you can say different different uh slabs where we just keep our hard disk. So if we want to duplicate our data within one data center, let's say one rack for one data and second rack for like the same data but this is within one data center that's called locally redundant storage. But if you want to make a duplicate copy of your data within different location within you can say different zone that's called zone redundant storage that means in one zone we have one data center and in second zone we have second data center. So your one copy will reside in one data center of one region like one zone and second copy of the same data in the second zone that's called ZRS zone redundant storage simple. The third one is region and I think now you are smart enough you would have guessed it like what does it mean? So first of all we saw locally redundant storage in which one data center then we have zone redundant in which we have multiple zones. Then as name suggest like Z region redundant storage I say GRS GRS geo redundant geo replication storage anything you can say so what it does it creates your one copy in one region and second copy in the second region and region can be let's say if you talk about Europe or let's say UK so UK south is one region UK west is another region region UK east is another region.
